# InfluxDB APM Plugin

The `influxdb` APM plugin allows querying metrics from an
[InfluxDB][influxdb_homepage] server. The plugin supports InfluxDB 1.x using
[InfluxQL][influxql_docs] queries.

## Agent Configuration Options

The plugin supports two authentication modes. Use HTTP basic authentication
when your InfluxDB server requires a username and password:

```hcl
apm "influxdb" {
  driver = "influxdb"

  config = {
    address  = "http://localhost:8086"
    database = "telegraf"

    username = "myuser"
    password = "mypassword"
  }
}
```

To use JWT authentication instead, provide `shared_secret` in place of
`password`. The plugin generates and refreshes tokens automatically:

```hcl
apm "influxdb" {
  driver = "influxdb"

  config = {
    address  = "http://localhost:8086"
    database = "telegraf"

    username      = "myuser"
    shared_secret = "my-influxdb-shared-secret"
    token_ttl     = "30m"   # optional, default: "1h"
  }
}
```

- `address` `(string: <required>)` - The absolute URL of the InfluxDB server,
  including scheme and port, for example `http://localhost:8086`.

- `database` `(string: <required>)` - The name of the InfluxDB database to
  query. You can also use `db` as a shorthand alias. When both are set,
  `database` takes precedence.

- `username` `(string: "")` - The username for authentication. Required when
  using `shared_secret` for JWT authentication.

- `password` `(string: "")` - The password to use for HTTP basic
  authentication.

- `shared_secret` `(string: "")` - The InfluxDB shared secret used to generate
  JWT tokens for authentication. The plugin automatically generates and refreshes
  tokens. The value must match the `INFLUXDB_HTTP_SHARED_SECRET` setting on the
  InfluxDB server. `shared_secret` requires `username` to be set and cannot be
  used together with `password`. The agent returns an error on startup if both
  are set.

- `token_ttl` `(string: "1h")` - The lifetime of auto-generated JWT tokens when
  using `shared_secret` authentication. Accepts Go duration strings, for example
  `"30m"`. The valid range is `1m` to `24h`.

- `version` `(string: "1")` - The InfluxDB API version to use. Only version
  `"1"` is supported. The agent returns an error on startup if any other value
  is set.

The InfluxDB plugin can also read its configuration options via environment
variables. The accepted keys are `INFLUXDB_ADDRESS`, `INFLUXDB_DATABASE`,
`INFLUXDB_USERNAME`, `INFLUXDB_PASSWORD`, and `INFLUXDB_SHARED_SECRET`. The
agent configuration parameters take precedence over the environment variables.

## Policy Configuration Options

```hcl
check {
  source = "influxdb"
  query  = "SELECT mean(usage_user) FROM cpu WHERE time >= now() - 2m"
  # ...
}
```

The `query` value must be a valid InfluxQL query. The plugin does not inject
time bounds automatically, so you must include a `WHERE time >= ...` clause to
limit the query window.

~> **Note:** The InfluxDB plugin does not support an instant `query_window`.
Use a duration-based window instead.

The plugin reads the metric value from a column named `value` in the query
result. If no `value` column is present, it uses the first non-`time` column
instead. This means you do not need to alias the result column to `value`:

```hcl
check {
  source = "influxdb"
  query  = "SELECT mean(usage_user) FROM cpu WHERE time >= now() - 2m GROUP BY time(10s)"
  # ...
}
```

When using `GROUP BY time()`, add `fill(none)` to your query to omit incomplete
trailing buckets instead of returning them as null values:

```sql
SELECT mean(usage_user) FROM cpu WHERE time >= now() - 2m GROUP BY time(10s) fill(none)
```

[influxdb_homepage]: https://www.influxdata.com/
[influxql_docs]: https://docs.influxdata.com/influxdb/v1/query_language/
